description: §23.5.2 — the SPEC's own advisory worked example `capabilities=[network("api.example.com")]` on the top-level `<program>` COMPILES CLEAN. It is an advisory, non-blocking declaration (§23.5.1); the capability token list is special-parsed (NOT a general expression), so `network` is a capability KEYWORD, never a scope ref. The regression anchor for the fail-closed-Nominal invariant: pre-impl-wave this hard-failed with a misleading fatal E-SCOPE-001 (`network` undeclared) plus a W-ATTR-001 unknown-attribute warning.
rationale: §23.5.1: a well-formed `capabilities=[…]` declaration is advisory and non-blocking — it MUST NOT hard-fail. The token list is recognized (E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN does not fire on a valid token), the attribute is registered (no W-ATTR-001), and its tokens never resolve as scope refs (no E-SCOPE-001). Only the incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED remains.
